title: phegopteris merrillii
intro: Phegopteris merrillii, also known as Merrill's maidenhair fern or maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Phegopteris merrillii include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain and swelling, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
